name: Enumerate the full legacy reader/writer breaking set (+ fold in pre-graph 401 fix)
description: First WP of PRG-06 (Identity Cutover). Statically enumerate the full breaking reader/writer surface (~868 ref sites / 77 non-test files upper bound) across the 5 legacy identity tables; do NOT discover endpoint-by-endpoint. Classify twin-exists/unbranched/missing. Missing twins (e.g. listProfilesV2) are ownership-scoping security-sensitive -> TDD. Output sequences WP-2..N + WP-FLAG. Fold in the pre-graph 401 fix (branch fix-v2-pregraph-401, commit de8df6e86) as slice-1. Canon wins; S0-S6 design choices are NOT canonical. Tracker: _wip/identity-cutover/execution-tracker.md (eduagent-build).
acceptanceCriteria: Complete static enumeration of every legacy-identity-table reader/writer and unbranched call site in non-test source, each classified: (a) twin-exists+branched=OK; (b) twin-exists+unbranched=wire-up; (c) no-twin=build (TDD, ownership-scoping security-sensitive). Output is the sizing+sequencing input for the downstream cutover WPs. Pre-graph 401 fix (de8df6e86) folded in as slice-1.
riskImpact: Diagnostic that sizes and sequences ALL downstream PRG-06 cutover WPs. Under-enumeration is the dominant risk: a missed legacy-identity-table reader survives to the re-run cutover and authenticated reads 500 post-DROP (the exact dovetail failure that broke the staging cutover), or an unbranched ownership-scoped reader with no *V2 twin (e.g. listProfilesV2) ships a cross-tenant/IDOR read. Mitigation: static whole-surface enumeration (not endpoint-by-endpoint) with explicit (a) twin+branched / (b) twin+unbranched / (c) no-twin classification, and security-sensitive missing twins flagged for TDD. Over-enumeration risk is low (wasted WP effort only). Output is the sizing+sequencing input gating WP-2..N + WP-FLAG + the terminal data half (WI-586).
